ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who posted the tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "date_posted" TEXT /* Date and time when the tweet was posted */,
  "favorites" INTEGER /* Number of favorites the tweet has received */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet has received */,
  "post_type" TEXT /* Type of the tweet (original, retweet, quote) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  "media_url" TEXT /* URL of any media attached to the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location where the tweet was posted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the political figure */,
  "handle" TEXT /* Twitter handle of the political figure */,
  "party" TEXT /* Political party of the political figure */,
  "district" TEXT /* District represented by the political figure */,
  "profile_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile page */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information about the political figure */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for contact */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number for contact */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL to the user's official website */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the social media platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet */,
  "favorites" INTEGER /* Number of favorites the tweet has received */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet has received */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate of the tweet */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the tweet */,
  "replies" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Number of impressions (views) the tweet has received */,
  "engagement_time" REAL /* Average time spent by users engaging with the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the content category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the content category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for hierarchical categorization) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the category is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_content" (
  "content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content association */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content category */,
  "relevance_score" REAL /* Score indicating how relevant the tweet is to the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_content_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES content_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_content_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_label" TEXT /* Label for the sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the sentiment analysis was performed */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the sentiment analysis */,
  "source_tool" TEXT /* Name of the tool or service used for sentiment analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_analysis_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scheduling" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each scheduling record */,
  "tweet_id" TEXT /* ID of the tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who scheduled the tweet */,
  "post_time" TEXT /* Scheduled post time for the tweet */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the scheduling (e.g., pending, posted, canceled) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the scheduling record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the scheduling record was last updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the scheduling */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scheduling_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scheduling_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "social_media_accounts" (
  "account_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each social media account */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who manages the account */,
  "platform" TEXT /* Social media platform (e.g., Twitter, Facebook) */,
  "account_name" TEXT /* Name of the social media account */,
  "followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers of the account */,
  "profile_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile page on the social media platform */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information about the account */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the account is verified */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date when the account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("account_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_social_media_accounts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "platform_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each platform metric */,
  "account_id" INTEGER /* ID of the social media account */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., followers, likes) */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the metric (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_platform_metrics_account_id FOREIGN KEY ("account_id") REFERENCES social_media_accounts ("account_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trending_topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trending topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the trending topic */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the topic's trending period */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the topic's trending period */,
  "popularity_score" REAL /* Popularity score of the topic */,
  "platform" TEXT /* Social media platform where the topic is trending */,
  "trend_rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the topic in the trending list */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the trending data (e.g., Twitter API, Facebook Insights) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)
